channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: FBI warns New Jersey residents not to shoot down drones or point lasers at aircraft
item.description: Recent drone sightings have prompted a collective sense of panic from some residents, despite assurances from U.S. intelligence agencies and the Defense Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump chooses Herschel Walker as US ambassador to Bahamas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Herschel Walker, the football legend who unsuccessfully ran for a U.S. Senate seat in Georgia, has been chosen by President-elect Donald Trump to be ambassador to the Bahamas. Trump made the announcement Tuesday in a post on Truth Social, his social media website.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Planes, stars and hobbyists: Lawmakers insist nothing ‘nefarious’ is happening in NJ skies
item.description: Some Republicans in New Jersey are working on new legislation to defend against drones despite repeated assurances from senior U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After investigating Jan. 6, House GOP sides with Trump and goes after Liz Cheney
item.description: Wrapping up their report on the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, House Republicans have concluded it’s former GOP Rep. Liz Cheney who should be prosecuted for her work probing the riot.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas school districts asked to return $16 million in federal funding for special education services
item.description: The request comes after an audit found Texas improperly billed the federal government for reimbursements related to Medicaid-eligible students.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Attorney for RFK Jr. blasts 'hysterical' media report as distortion of HHS pick's views on vaccines
item.description: Aaron Siri, adviser to RFK Jr., pushes back against a New York Times report about him as mischaracterizing the HHS secretary nominee's stand on polio vaccines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Farm state Republicans appear skeptical about RFK amid his quest for HHS confirmation
item.description: Some rural Republicans are hoping Trump HHS secretary pick RFK Jr. will provide some clarification about his stances on farming and food production.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: The Register's parent Gannett Co. on Tuesday dismissed the lawsuit as meritless and said it would vigorously defend its First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator moves to block feds from disposing of border wall materials amid auction backlash
item.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty, R-Tenn. is introducing legislation to stop the federal government from auctioning border wall materials at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archivist says Equal Rights Amendment can't be certified as Democrats push Biden to recognize it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a rare joint statement, the archivist and deputy archivist of the United States said Tuesday that the 1970s-era Equal Rights Amendment cannot be certified without further action by Congress or the courts, as Democrats press President Joe Biden to act unilaterally on its ratifica
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Grand jury indicts UnitedHealthcare CEO suspect on first-degree murder
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg (D) announced Tuesday that a grand jury handed up an indictment against Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO. Mangione faces 11 charges, including first-degree murder and two counts of second-degree murder.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden’s Cabinet officials stand by statements of support as term draws to a close
item.description: With a month left in office, Biden’s Cabinet reaffirms support, stating confidence in his leadership and fitness despite ongoing controversy and debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's transition team eyes expansion of ankle monitors for illegal immigrants not in custody
item.description: The incoming Trump administration is looking at potentially expanding the number of illegal immigrants on GPS monitoring, while also extending the amount of time they are monitored.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Andrew Lipow, president of consulting firm Lipow Oil Associates, said a 25 percent tariff amounts to a surcharge of about 40 cents per gallon.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: FBI should probe 'potential' Liz Cheney 'witness tampering' in Jan 6 matter, House Republicans say
item.description: The House Administration Subcommittee on Oversight released its report investigating the alleged failings of the Jan. 6 committee, calling for an investigation into former Rep.Cheney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kamala Harris tells students to 'stay in the fight' after the election
item.description: The vice president's remarks to students in the Maryland Corps service year program were her most extensive since her concession speech. She did not disclose her own plans for after her term ends, but she spoke about “our shared commitment to the work ahead.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s lawyers allege juror misconduct in latest bid to get his hush money conviction dismissed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers are alleging that his hush money conviction was tainted by juror misconduct, opening a new front in their fight to overturn the verdict and throw out the historic case.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congress unveils funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: Congressional leaders have unveiled legislation that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Connolly bests Ocasio-Cortez in key vote to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Monday claimed the first round of the two-step contest to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, besting Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding bill will run through March, include $100B in disaster aid
item.description: Pieces of a bipartisan effort to prevent a government shutdown during the holiday season are beginning to come into focus, including a March end date and $100 billion in disaster aid. GOP leadership briefed Republicans on Tuesday morning on some of the details of the forthcoming legislation.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Biden calls for ban on congressional stock trading
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden endorsed a ban on congressional stock trading in an interview that’s being released this week, belatedly weighing in on an issue that has been debated on Capitol Hill for years.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In disputed Shakopee-area legislative race, voters share what was on uncounted ballots
item.description: While 20 ballots believed to have been discarded before being counted won’t be recovered, some voters who cast those missing ballots shared what was on them. They appear to show Rep. Brad Tabke’s narrow lead would stand up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Americans end 2024 with grim economic outlook, but Republicans are optimistic for 2025: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The unemployment rate is healthy and the stock market is up, but Democrats are feeling more pessimistic about the U.S. economy after Donald Trump's election victory, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP lawmaker files bill banning political flags in public buildings
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote in coming days
item.description: The Senate on Monday cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), teeing up a final vote in the coming days before Congress wraps up its work for the year.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says it was 'stupid' for Biden to let Ukraine use US weapons to strike deeper into Russia
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Monday suggested that he may reverse President Joe Biden's recent decision to allow Ukrainian forces to use American long-range weapons to strike deeper into Russian territory. Trump called the decision made by Biden last month “stupid.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill to officially make bald eagle national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: A bill to officially designate the bald eagle as the national bird will head to President Biden’s desk after passage in the House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trial over Minnesota House race that could swing control of chamber focuses on lost ballots
item.description: Republicans challenged the election result arguing that a 14-vote margin between the DFL representative who won and his GOP opponent could be made up in a pool of 20 ballots that were accidentally tossed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation
